avalancheActivity.highlights: In regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations are prevalent and the danger is level 4 (high).
avalancheActivity.comment:
A very large quantity of fresh snow as well as the sometimes deep wind slabs are very prone to triggering in all aspects. The fresh snow and the wind slabs can be released naturally. In these regions numerous large and, in isolated cases, very large avalanches are to be expected.
From starting zones where no previous releases have taken place in particular the avalanches can reach as far as the valley bottom and endanger transportation routes that are exposed.

Without question, it is inadvisable to engage in ski touring, freeriding and snowshoe hiking.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 100 to 150 cm of snow, and up to 180 cm in some localities, has fallen since yesterday.
The new snow is bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
The moderate wind has transported the new snow.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Large quantities of fresh snow and the wind-drifted snow remain for the foreseeable future prone to triggering.

avalancheActivity.highlights: At intermediate and high altitudes a high avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The large quantity of fresh snow and the wind slabs represent the main danger. Over a wide area new snow is lying on a hard crust. The new snow is poorly bonded.
In all aspects and at intermediate and high altitudes medium-sized and large avalanches are to be expected as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.
Avalanches can reach valley bottoms and in isolated cases endanger exposed transportation routes.

Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and careful route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 100 cm of snow, and up to 130 cm in some localities, has fallen since Saturday.
The new snow is bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
The moderate wind has transported the new snow.

The old snowpack is well consolidated and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche danger will not decrease for the time being.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Outside marked and open pistes a dangerous avalanche situation will persist.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The new snow of the last two days can be released easily or naturally in all aspects and generally at intermediate and high altitudes.
Including in starting zones in which avalanches have already been released and at intermediate and high altitudes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are to be expected.

Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for extensive experience in the assessment of avalanche danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Saturday.
The moderate wind has transported the new snow.
The new snow is poorly bonded with the old snowpack in many places and generally at intermediate and high altitudes.

In particular at intermediate and high altitudes a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche activity will gradually decrease.

avalancheActivity.highlights: New snow and wind slabs: In these regions the avalanche danger is considerable (level 3).
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area new snow is lying on a hard crust. The new snow is poorly bonded.
The covering of new snow is unstable.
The avalanches can over a wide area be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach large size in isolated cases.

Ski touring and other off-piste activities, including snowshoe hiking, call for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and careful route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Saturday.
The new snow is bonding only slowly with the old snowpack.
The moderate wind has transported the new snow.

The old snowpack is well consolidated and its surface consists of loosely bonded snow lying on a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. Intermediate and high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The avalanche danger will not decrease for the time being.
